author: Sofia Martínez
title: Pest Control Local SEO: Rank for High-Intent Service Calls
description: Pest control companies leave 60% of local leads on the table. Master local SEO, Google Ads, and review management to capture emergency service calls.

sections.p: A pest control company in Atlanta was spending $4,200 per month on Google Ads and getting 12 leads. Their organic search visibility was near-zero. Within four months of fixing their local SEO—Google Business optimization, citation cleanup, location page structure—they went from 2 organic leads per month to 18. Ad spend stayed the same, but they added an entire revenue channel. Pest control is a high-intent local service. When someone searches "termites near me" or "emergency pest control," they're ready to call. The problem: most pest control companies ignore SEO and rely entirely on paid ads, missing 30-40% of available demand.

Generic keywords like "pest control" have no local intent. Target the queries your customers actually search. Rank for: "bed bug treatment [neighborhood]," "termite inspection [zip code]," "rodent removal same day," "wasp nest removal [city]." These keywords have 100-600 monthly searches and 70%+ conversion intent because they're location-specific and problem-specific.

A pest control franchise with 8 service areas created 24 landing pages (3 per location): one for rodent control, one for bed bugs, one for termites. Each page targeted local keywords plus the specific pest type. By month three, they ranked on page one for 47 local keywords and generated 240 organic service calls across all locations in a single month. Average service call value: $320. That's $76,800 in organic-sourced revenue from landing page optimization alone.

sections.h2: Lock Down Google Business Profile Authority

sections.p: For pest control, Google Business appearance directly influences service calls. A profile with 50+ reviews, consistent hours, professional photos, and regular posts generates 40% more calls than an incomplete profile. Update your profile weekly with pest-specific content: seasonal warnings, treatment updates, industry news.

list: Add 15+ high-quality photos: technicians treating pest issues, before/after treatment photos, office team, service vehicles with company branding, treatment chemicals/equipment

Post every 5-7 days during peak season (spring/summer for most regions) with actionable content: "3 signs of carpenter ant infestation," "Why winter rodent control matters," "Safe pet-friendly termite treatment options"
Add service categories for each pest type: Bed Bug Extermination, Termite Control, Rodent Removal, Mosquito Treatment, Wasp Removal
Enable booking if you offer same-day estimates; this alone increases conversion by 18-25%
Respond to every review—positive and negative—within 12 hours. Response rate affects ranking visibility in the map pack

sections.quote: A pest control company with 92 Google reviews and 4.8 stars will beat a company with 12 reviews and 5 stars. Google rewards recency and volume.

sections.h2: Clean Up and Build Citations Systematically

sections.p: Citation errors kill local SEO for service businesses. If your company name, address, or phone number is inconsistent across 20 directories, Google loses confidence in your location data. Pest control companies often list on: Yelp, Angie's List, HomeAdvisor, Better Business Bureau, local chamber of commerce, Google Maps, Apple Maps, and regional aggregators. One company we audited had 7 different phone numbers listed across directories. That fragmentation cost them an estimated 30-35% of potential local visibility.

Start with an audit: search for your company name + "my business" across Google, Yelp, and Angie's List. Document every listing you find. Standardize all name, address, phone, and website information. Then, add your business to 5-8 high-authority local directories specific to home services (HomeServe, Care.com, Thumbtack, Servicewise). The cost is typically $30-100 per directory per year. One franchisee client reported a 220% increase in location-based call volume after standardizing citations and adding to 6 new authoritative directories.

Pest problems are seasonal. Spring brings termites and ants. Summer means mosquitoes and wasps. Fall is rodent season. Winter is bed bugs (indoor heating attracts them). Create blog content targeting these seasonal queries 6-8 weeks before peak season. A post on "rodent control preparation" published in July will rank by October when searches spike 280%.

list: February-March: Create content around spring pest prevention, termite season, carpenter ant warnings
April-May: Target mosquito control, outdoor pest prevention, natural pest deterrents for patios
July-August: Publish about yellow jacket identification, wasp nest removal, summer pest treatment
September-October: Focus on rodent prevention, pest-proofing homes, commercial pest management for Q4
November-December: Target bed bug awareness, winter pest infestations, holiday pest prevention

sections.h2: Combine SEO With Smart Google Ads Strategy

sections.p: SEO and paid ads work together. Use Google Ads to dominate high-intent keywords immediately (within hours), while you build organic rankings (3-6 months). Most pest control companies bid on generic keywords like "pest control" ($12-18 per click) and waste budget. Instead, bid on emergency keywords with 2-4 competitors: "bed bugs treatment now," "termite inspection today," "rodent removal same day." These convert at 15-22% and cost $4-8 per click.

A Houston pest control company shifted their Google Ads strategy from broad keywords to 40+ location-specific, emergency-intent keywords. Average cost per lead dropped 43% ($8.20 to $4.60), and conversion rate increased from 6% to 16%. They maintained the same monthly budget but tripled lead volume. Combine this with organic SEO for location pages, and you've built a lead generation machine that doesn't depend on a single channel.
